meta.title: Digital Content Creator Pathways | Who's to Say ? Foundation
meta.description: Build confidence and real-world skills in digital media, design, photography, and studio production.

hero.title: Digital Content Creator Pathways
hero.subtitle: Video, design, photography, and production skills—built one project at a time, at the participant's pace.
hero.bestFor: Participants who are interested in content creation and storytelling through media.

blocks.html: Digital Content Creator Pathways helps young adults build confidence and real-world skills in digital media, design, and technology. The focus is not on "one-size-fits-all job training," but on discovering each person's interests and translating them into meaningful, practical skills.

items: Creating Clips, Using Templates, and simple effects
Exporting and safely sharing finished videos
Creating graphics in Canva or similar tools
Stickers, simple posters, social media images
Basic visual branding concepts (color, layout, readability)
Using a smartphone or DSLR camera
Photo Assistant Basics, lighting, and simple editing
Building a "portfolio" of images for a show
Basics of microphones, lighting, and camera setup
Being on camera vs. behind the camera
Working as part of a creative "crew"
Increased comfort with technology and media tools
Improved communication and planning (following steps, finishing projects)
A small digital portfolio that can be shared with families, coordinators, or potential employers
Experience working toward deadlines and goals

getStarted.title: Get Started
getStarted.prompt: Want to explore a Digital Path fit?

blocks.html: Sessions are delivered as individualized or small-group coaching sessions. Participants work on projects that matter to them: editing a gaming clip, designing a logo, recording a podcast, taking photos, and building small content pieces they can be proud to share.

Goods & Services-funded sessions may include coaching in accessible creative tools (video editing, digital design, photography, and basic studio production). Supports are individualized and goal-driven, building communication, planning, and practical digital skills based on the person's interests.
